what_it_does: HTML5 entry point that loads two stylesheets, the React 18 UMD runtime and ReactDOM from a CDN, Babel standalone for JSX transpilation, and eight JSX component files to mount a React app into <div id="app">.
observations: A <!-- @dsCard ... --> comment at the top carries metadata: group, name, subtitle, and viewport 1280x720.
React 18.3.1 and ReactDOM 18.3.1 are loaded as UMD development builds with integrity hashes.
Babel standalone 7.29.0 is loaded for in-browser JSX transformation.
Eight component scripts are loaded with type="text/babel": HubBug, MenuButton, QuickMenu, InfoPanel, SharePanel, FloorplanOverlay, CaptureComment, and HubApp.
No inline JavaScript or explicit module system is present.

The body contains only
before scripts.
standouts: The @dsCard metadata comment encodes design-system handoff context directly in the HTML.
It uses CDN React development builds plus Babel standalone, implying a build-free preview/demo setup.
The ordered list of JSX components suggests a deliberate dependency loading sequence ending with HubApp.
conventions: Standard HTML5 document structure with lang="en" and charset UTF-8.
External stylesheets in <head>, scripts at the end of <body>.
Subresource Integrity attributes on CDN scripts.
JSX files served through Babel's text/babel script type.
concerns: Development-only React builds are used, which are not optimized for production.
Babel standalone transpiles JSX in the browser, which is slower and less robust than a build step.
Script loading order is implicit; a missing or reordered file could break mounting.
No <noscript> fallback or CDN fallback is provided.
No root error boundary or loading state is visible in this file.
notes: This file is the CDN-based React bootstrap shell for the Virtual Hub UI kit handoff, wiring global/local styles and eight JSX components into #app.
questions: Is this file intended for static preview, developer integration, or documentation only?
Are the script tags ordered by explicit dependency graph, or just convention?
Is there a separate build pipeline that this preview shell shadows?
Why are development React builds chosen over production builds?
What does HubApp render and how does it receive state?
purpose_vs_actual: The @dsCard subtitle states a buyer-side panotour overlay with MENU, Info, Floorplan, and Capture; the file itself is the HTML shell that loads the React components for that overlay, so the purpose and actual content align.
apparent_themes: Rapid, build-free prototyping with CDN dependencies
Separation of style tokens and React components
intention_signals: It appears designed to let reviewers or developers open a single HTML file and immediately see the Virtual Hub UI kit rendered, without needing a local build toolchain.
novelty: Embedding structured @dsCard metadata inside an HTML comment and using Babel standalone to compile JSX directly in a design-system handoff context.
contribution: Defines the Virtual Hub UI kit entry surface by declaring which stylesheets and React/JSX modules must be loaded to bootstrap the overlay viewer.
summary_for_embedding: index.html is an HTML5 entry point for the ConceptV Virtual Hub UI Kit that loads global and local stylesheets, React 18 UMD runtime and Babel standalone from a CDN, and mounts a React app from eight JSX component files into a div with id app.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to provide a quick, build-free preview shell for the buyer-side panotour overlay UI kit components.
